dialogue.utterance: First units to a reported unconscious male at 14423 Bald Hill Street, [CITY].
Hello, I'm Sarah, an EMT. Can you tell me what brought you to call 911 today?
He was found unconscious in a chair at home. I couldn't wake him.
No response to voice-checking eye opening now.
No eye opening. Pinpoint pupils bilaterally, non-reactive to light.
No verbal response-still assessing motor responses to pain.
No motor response to pain stimulation. GCS is 7: eye 2, verbal 1, motor 4.
Airway is patent-no immediate risk of airway compromise at this time.
Applying non-rebreather at 15 LPM. He's resisting it slightly.
Respiratory rate 9-shallow and labored. Watch for worsening or airway support needs.
Pulse is 80, strong and regular-no evidence of tachycardia at this time.
Blood pressure is 111/71-no hypotension observed at this stage.
Oxygen saturation is 97%-mildly decreased, but no hypoxia at this point.
Blood glucose is 176-no hypoglycemia, but fasting cannot be excluded yet.
Applying 4-lead EKG now. Rhythm is sinus with normal rate.
Airway is still patent. Reassessing breathing and oxygenation now.
Respiratory rate is now 10. Labored but not distressing.
Pulse has increased slightly to 95-watch for progression.
Blood pressure is now 187/88-elevated systolic. No signs of shock.
GCS remains low. Will reassess after Narcan administration.
Administering 0.5 mg Narcan intranasally now for suspected opioid overdose.
EKG rechecked-sinus rhythm remains, no acute changes.
Respiratory rate is 12-tachypneic but improving slightly since Narcan.
Heart rate is up to 108-mild tachycardia, likely due to Narcan effect.
Blood pressure is now 160/93-still elevated, but no hypotension.
Oxygen saturation has increased slightly to 97%-breathing is more stable.
Skin is warm, pink, and dry-no rashes or swelling.
No DCAP-BTLS to head or neck. Pupils remain pinpoint and non-reactive.
Oral and nasal passages are clear-no secretions or obstruction.
Equal chest rise. Heard clear bilateral lung sounds-no wheezing or crackles.
Abdomen is soft, non-tender, and non-distended.
Peripheral pulses are strong and regular. No deformities or skin breaks.
Left AC 18-gauge IV is in place and patent for medication use during transport.
When was the last time he ate or drank something?
Around midday-no one saw him eat or drink since then.
Any prior medical conditions or history of substance use?
No known history. No illnesses or medications-first time this has happened.
Did he have any medications at home or take anything prior to this incident?
No medications found. He said he hadn't taken anything recently.
There's no report of near-fainting. He just went from sitting to unconscious in less than five minutes.
Breathing rate now improved to 17-still tachypneic but stable.
Blood pressure is now 139/82-trending slightly downward from earlier.
SpO2 is stable at 99%-no sign of hypoxia.
Glucose remains undetectable-no concerns due to stable mental status.
[COMPANY_NAME] will be the destination-neurology or overdose protocol likely needed.
Patient secured to stretcher using harness. All restraints confirmed tight.
GCS improved to 15 after Narcan. Patient is now alert and oriented.
All restraints double-checked. Equipment is secure. Ready for ambulance transport.
Following protocol 7-1 for opioid overdose. All actions per standing orders and assessment.
[CITY] is the confirmed facility. ED has agreed to take the case.
Heart rate now 91-slowly trending back to baseline.
Breathing 17, still tachypneic but less labored-likely due to prior opioid depression.
Blood pressure remains at 139/82-no significant change from last reading.
Oxygen saturation remains stable at 99%-no signs of respiratory decompensation.
Blood sugar still zero-fasting suspected but not contributing to the instability.
Rhythm remains sinus-no new findings.
Gear secured in ambulance. All restraints verified at secondary check before loading.
Oxygen, EKG, and IV are run. Equipment online. Patient alert and stable.
Estimated arrival at hospital in five minutes. ALS support is already at the ED entrance.
Transfer discussed with hospital. ED has reserved a bed, per protocol.
This is a previously healthy male with new onset altered mental status and opioid use history.
All restraints latched tight. No movement noted during final check.
Puls activity remains stable-checking more oxygen and EKG now.
Hospital arrival confirmed. ALS ready at the door.
EKG and IV remain active. Patient is awake and cooperative.
ED nurse has taken over care. All systems still active. GCS is 15, alert and oriented.
EMS transfer complete. All care is now the responsibility of ED staff.
All interventions and vitals documented on ePCR.
